transcription.text: When you ask an agent to make motion graphics from a design.md, it often builds a presentation.
That's not random. Most design specs were written for websites and products.
They describe buttons, cards, navs, grids, spacing, but your agent needs different instructions.
How long a message can live, what should move, what needs scale, what deserves silence.
So we built frame.md. It takes the brand language from design.md
and translates it into video native direction. Paste a design spec, hyperframes reads it,
interprets it, and generates a frame language your agent can actually direct from.
A paragraph like hero band with spotlight and terminal stops being documentation.
It becomes a scene plan. Without it, agents tend to arrange the brand like an interface.
With it, they compose the brand like a film. This is not a prettier design file. It's a new format
for agents. Design.md tells us what the brand is. Frame.md tells us how it moves.
